operating_state: Prologis generated $4.9 billion in rental revenues for 2022, up from $4.1 billion driven by 48.0% rent change on rollover and robust same-store growth in tight logistics markets. The October Duke acquisition added 494 industrial properties expanding the consolidated operating portfolio to 2,812 properties with 595 million square feet at 98.3% occupancy. Net earnings attributable to common stockholders increased to $3.359 billion from $2.934 billion, while Core FFO rose to $4.188 billion from $3.172 billion reflecting strong operational performance.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased reflecting the expanded portfolio from the Duke acquisition and inflationary pressures on operating costs. Development activities faced higher construction costs due to inflationary environment, labor shortages, and supply chain disruptions that exceeded original budgets. Administrative expenses and interest expense increased driven by assuming Duke debt at 4.9% weighted average rate and inflationary increases.
material_events: Prologis completed the Duke acquisition in October 2022, assuming debt and issuing senior notes. The company generated $2.7 billion in net proceeds from property contributions to co-investment ventures and dispositions, realizing gains. Strategic Capital earned promotes primarily from PELF in Europe during the third quarter based on asset valuations and cumulative returns. The company terminated its 2019 Global Facility and established a new facility with extended maturity to June 2026.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency exposure intensified with approximately 11.6% of total consolidated assets invested in non-U.S. dollar currencies, including Euro and Japanese yen debt. The strengthening U.S. dollar created translation headwinds, though the company realized offsetting gains from undesignated derivative settlements. Inflationary pressures and steeply rising interest rates created challenging capital market conditions, while asset valuations in co-investment ventures declined in the second half with additional declines expected.
forward_outlook: Management expects some decline in asset valuations and will maintain disciplined capital deployment, focusing on build-to-suit developments while pausing contributions to open-ended funds near-term. Development portfolio requires additional investment through November 2024 completion. The company anticipates organic revenue growth from increased market rents and high lease mark-to-market positioning, with total available liquidity providing financial flexibility.
uncertainty_profile: Development portfolio completion risk requires significant additional investment over two years with execution dependent on market conditions and construction cost inflation. Co-investment venture debt refinancing uncertainty exists with third-party debt requiring future refinancing. The company faces potential REIT qualification risks under complex requirements including maintaining distribution ratios and satisfying quarterly asset tests, while environmental liabilities exist across the portfolio with exposure to hazardous substances from tenant operations.
